displayTitle: Five Questions Every Echo Must Survive

beats.narration: Welcome to Level Two, the evaluation level. You sign for what an indication is, so every echo must be explainable from first principles.

payload.title: EVALUATION LEVEL
payload.sub: Level II: you sign for what an indication is.

beats.narration: Your toolkit is Level One physics: velocity converts time to distance, geometry maps the beam path, impedance decides what every boundary reflects.
Five questions. What mode? What velocity? Where in time does it sit? What impedance step explains the amplitude? Does it move correctly with the probe?

payload.heading: Every signal walks the chain
payload.note: FIVE QUESTIONS · EVERY SIGNAL MUST ANSWER

beats.narration: Impostors fail the chain: dead-zone clutter, wrap-around ghosts, mode-converted arrivals, grain noise. A reportable indication is the signal that passes all five.
So here's the rule: explain every echo from first principles, then sign. That habit is the difference between Level One and Level Two.
